Among those working within the court system is newly appointed Judge Lola Carmichael, a highly regarded and impressive deputy district attorney who doesn't intend to sit back on the bench in her new role, but instead leans in, immediately pushing the boundaries and challenging the expectations of what a judge can be. Those offering Lola counsel are her roguish best friend and colleague, Mark Callan, a successful deputy district attorney who now must adjust to the shifting boundaries in their personal and professional relationship; Supervisory Judge Lisa Benner, an influential, powerful figure tasked with overseeing Lola's transition into her new job; and Lola's efficient, pragmatic judicial assistant, Sherri Kansky.

programme.desc: Jack uses his contacts to dig for dirt, Bernie returns to Walford, and Teddy gets an unwelcome blast from the past.

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An entrepreneur unveils a medical device that was born out of a brush with death.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The Doc clashes with the new district midwife, whose natural methods put Louisa's health at risk. Bert takes a shine to a new employee at the restaurant, but her culinary preferences leave something to be desired.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: With guests Lucy Beaumont, Anton Du Beke, Desiree Burch and Ralf Little.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location Australia
programme.desc: This week Veronica and Bryce are heading to Canberra, to help two couples trying to get a leg up on the property ladder. Gab and Monique are making the biggest move of their lives, with three kids and a dog in tow, they're swapping Alice Springs for the nation's capital. But big moves don't come without big wish lists, and things don't always go according to plan. Meanwhile, long distance lovers Tarun and Mel have spent the last three years apart, now they want to buy their first home together. The budget is tight, but that's not stopping them from having big dreams, and it's Bryce and Veronica's job to find the middle ground!

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Terri and Paul are selling a main house and guest house on a large waterfront block on Lake Macquarie, north of Sydney. They're asking $1.2 million, but have been four years on the market with three agents, so something isn't working. It's very dated and multi-coloured and in need of a face lift to meet the market. The couple need to move on as they're keen to go caravanning while Terri still can (he has early-onset dementia).

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Former infantryman Ben bought this modest weatherboard in New Norfolk, northwest of Hobart, in 2008, confident it was an idyllic place to raise his family but when his wife Claire's father died suddenly in 2010, they decided to move back to the mainland closer to family and quickly bought in Victoria. They put New Norfolk on the market where it's remained on/off for five years, bringing in a modest rent. With four children, Ben is struggling with the two mortgages and works most weekends, missing out on the kids' sports etc. With a new agent on the case, it's time to upgrade New Norfolk and offload it once and for all.

programme.title: Love It Or List It Australia
programme.desc: Empty nesters Karen & Paul are looking at their next chapter. Will they be dazzled by what Andrew has to show them or will Neale prove you don't have to move home to fall in love with it again?

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Cole and Jane are hippies at heart with a blended family of six kids. Owner-builders, they're working from a design they came up with themselves based on a heart with four chambers.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: When Alex and Danny Moore bought their three-bed semi, Danny thought it was with a view to doing up and selling on. But Alex has other ideas and sees this as the perfect place to start a family.

programme.title: Ugly House To Lovely House
programme.desc: Can George and architect Greg Blee help Tony and Sarah with their dated '70s pebbledash house? Greg has a plan to link to the landscape beyond, including a Shard-like extension.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: New Zealand's Best Homes
programme.desc: Phil gets a taste of the country life right in the middle of the city, before venturing to Queenstown to experience a modern masterpiece.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: When Lynne and Paul met while sailing five years ago, neither had plans to fall in love, let alone relocate to the wilds of Tasmania to build a home. But that all changes for the Melbourne couple when they visit the Apple Isle and find a beautiful stretch of coastal wilderness with panoramic views over the Tasman Sea. With grand plans to build a huge Modern home, they decide that Paul will project manage construction. But living onsite for months on end in a battered up old caravan will test his skills, endurance and ultimately his health.

programme.desc: Siobhan McSweeney hosts Music Week as the potters are challenged to sculpt a life like bust of a music legend, kick starting with a surprise special message from Dolly Parton.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Steve and Moira are siblings, but only found each other as adults. Then, at their mother's funeral, they discovered they have a sister. Lisa is searching for her brother, who she also only learned of as an adult.

programme.title: Million Pound…
programme.desc: Simon and Julie take daughter Lily and their dogs to Croatia. Delanie plans to rent out her cherished motorhome. Erick and Krystle showcase their converted bus at a festival.

programme.title: DIY SOS The Big Build
programme.desc: DIY SOS with Rhod Gilbert transforms a 2.5 acre site in Brandesburton into an adventure campsite for St Michael's Youth Project, supporting local children and families with BBC Children in Need.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: New Zealand's Best Homes

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Furniture maker Lucinda, son Dan and daughter Rosie build a wooden armadillo house. They need to find innovative ways to transport materials without damaging tree roots without heavy machinery.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Mary and Tom were looking to sell after Mary was diagnosed with a degenerative disease that affects her mobility. But after a year on the market, the couple need help so they can downsize to a more suitable home.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Property in the coastal town of Austinmer is so hot that buying another house before you sell is considered the norm. Judy and Graham Mahoney never dreamed they would have trouble selling their three-bedroom weatherboard cottage, so they went ahead and purchased a new home before they sold. After 10 months on the market, a failed auction, and 80 inspections, they have a big problem and an even bigger bridging loan. Buyers are turned off by the bad presentation, poor level of maintenance, and the lack of a communal bathroom. Despite the feedback, Judy and Graham refuse to budge from their $700,000 asking price. They need the money to renovate their new home, but as each month passes, the couple slip further into debt. They have loans on both properties and need to find almost $6000 each month just to stay afloat.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan and the team are creating a garden dedicated to the celebration of all things textural. Full of plants that you want to stroke, touch and feel, this garden is being created for a hardworking support worker.

programme.title: Christmas At Stately Home
programme.desc: The story of how Castle Howard gets their estate ready for the Christmas period.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: In Week three, the sewers tackle travel-themed challenges: a summer blouse from tablecloths, nautical bags in the Transformation, and Riviera-inspired outfits in Made to Measure. Who will impress?

programme.title: Celebrity Sewing Bee Festive Specials
programme.desc: The Vivienne, Lesley Joseph, Sabrina Grant and Sally Philips must make a child's party skirt in the pattern challenge and turn old clothes into a recognisable pantomime character in the transformation challenge. Finally, for the made to measure challenge they are asked to create a New Year's Eve party dress that evokes memories of a particular New Year's Eve, with surprising results.

programme.title: Inside The Ambulance
programme.desc: Join the paramedics as they treat a woman trapped in her car after a crash, a man with a hernia and a poorly three-year-old. Plus, they're called to an elderly woman who's badly cut her legs.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Emergency
programme.desc: Mark is rushed to A&E struggling to breathe. Thomas is in with a high temperature that may be linked to his rare blood condition and Katrina is treated after she injured her knee climbing over a fence.

programme.title: Sex, Mind And the Menopause
programme.desc: Davina McCall explores how menopause can affect the mind as well as the body, with memory loss and brain fog decimating women at work. And the latest on hormone therapy and women and testosterone.

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex is hit hard by the struggles of patients going through IVF as she meets Jasmine and Rumen who are trying for their second child, after Rumen's cancer and Jasmine's previous miscarriage.

programme.title: Home Greek Home
programme.desc: Sarah Moore follows her dream of buying a house in Greece, but has never seen it in person. Upon arriving, Sarah discovers the complications of renovations in a country with different building rules and regulations.

programme.title: Grand Designs House of the Year
programme.desc: This episode looks at five longlisted houses showcasing exceptional materials and craftsmanship, before revealing the winner of the 2022 prize.

programme.desc: In the lovingly restored Chateau de Vezins in France, we meet a guest whose daily health routine puts the cat among the pigeons. On top of that, the private chef has left, leaving the cooking to the guest.

programme.desc: Jay and the team get a unique bicycle sidecar back on the road again, restore what was a Victorian sewing table, and tackle a drum which has seen front line action in both world wars.

programme.title: Alone
programme.desc: In order to endure the Arctic for 100 days, the participants start building shelters that can make it for the long haul. They turn their attention to procuring food, drawing the attention of dangerous predators.

programme.title: Paranormal Survivor
programme.desc: Carly Hall is haunted by evil spirits from her nightmares, a couple are attacked by a demon in their dreams and Ivan Latendresse is by ghosts that implore him to hurt others and take his own life.

programme.title: Haunted Case Files
programme.desc: Things turn ugly inside a salon for an investigator and his team; a ghost hunter discovers that a remote island is alive with history; investigators unleash evil while exploring a historic home.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Alone
programme.desc: In order to endure the Arctic for 100 days, the participants start building shelters that can make it for the long haul. They turn their attention to procuring food, drawing the attention of dangerous predators.

programme.desc: Greg Davies challenges his five fearful funny people to entertain him by completing more baffling challenges, which involve corneas, crackers and construction.

While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.sub-title: A Tiger Chase And A Tight Place
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Carl Andrews gets behind the wheel to transport a sizeable load, Russell's mobile classroom is shaken, and the Ferrises manage to dodge raging bush fires.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: It is two years earlier: in Camp Toccoa, GA, volunteers train for America's new military experiment, the paratroops. Theirs is one of nine companies in the 506th Parachute Infantry Regiment: E, aka Easy Company, commanded by the domineering Lt. Herbert Sobel. Through a regimen that involves frequent six-mile climbs up and down Currahee, a local mountain, Sobel succeeds in drilling the men into an elite unit. But his heavy-handed tactics, punishments for petty or imagined infractions, and strategic failures during manoeuvres, alienate Sobel from his men, who prefer his cool-headed second-in-command, Lt. Winters. After the 506th is shipped to England to complete training, Sobel harasses Winters by citing him for failing to inspect a latrine on time. When Winters requests a trial by court-martial, six of his sergeants: Salty Harris, Chuck Grant, Bill Guarnere, Carwood Lipton, Mike Ranney and Floyd Talbert request transfer rather than follow Sobel into combat.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Landing in a field, Winters finds he has lost his rifle and supply bag. He teams up with a private from another company, John Hall, and sets out to find their units. During the night they link up with a few more soldiers, and ambush a horse-drawn German supply convoy, their first enemy encounter. Regrouping in a nearby town, Winters runs into Easy's Lt. 'Buck' Compton, who tells him that Lt. Meehan is unaccounted for, which puts Winters in charge. His orders are to lead an attack on a nearby cluster of German artillery pieces endangering the seaborne infantry at Utah Beach. Winters deploys a small group on the entrenched enemy positions, and eventually takes four artillery pieces in succession, disabling them with TNT. The euphoria is tempered, however, when Winters finds Hall dead, killed by machine-gun fire. The mission is successful, but Winters has lost his first man as acting company commander.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Harry Welsh delivers Easy's next orders: take Carentan, the only town where armoured vehicles from Omaha and Utah beaches can link up. Winters briefs the men, and Easy heads into Carentan, encountering fierce resistance from Germans holed up in buildings. The town is eventually secured, although Winters gets hit above his ankle by ricochet gunfire.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: The respite doesn't last long; in September the men learn they're being airlifted to Holland as part of Operation Market-Garden, a joint American-British offensive that aims at securing quick access to Germany and ending the war by Christmas. Unlike Normandy, the daytime parachute drop is executed smoothly, and the company is greeted as heroes as they enter the town of Eindhoven after minimal resistance. All that changes when the company marches to Nuenen, where Easy is met by a brutal attack that ends with Compton wounded, a retreat ordered, and 'Bull' Randleman stranded in the town overnight. It's a sobering and bloody initiation to combat for the replacements.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As Winters labours over a report on the dike mission, Heyliger leads a rescue of British soldiers escaping from the besieged town of Arnhem. An anxious Winters worries about Easy (now one of three companies he helps command), especially after Heyliger is shot and wounded by a nervous sentry. After moving off the line to France, Nixon insists that Winters take a break and see Paris. Winters is haunted there by the memory of a young German he killed at close range on the dike. As he returns to the Company at Mourmelon, news comes of a massive German counterattack in the Ardennes Forest. Winters helps Easy race there to hold the line, his men ill-equipped for the cold and the battle ahead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Medic Eugene Roe is overwhelmed, on edge and close to combat exhaustion, when he finds friendship with a Belgian nurse. Easy Company spends a miserable Christmas in the trenches, but is buoyed after receiving the news that the German army's demand for surrender was met with General McAuliffe's defiant answer: "Nuts!"

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Several are killed and wounded in fierce shelling, compounded by the incompetence of their new commander, Lieutenant Dike, about whom Winters can do nothing. Easy takes Foy, but at an enormous cost.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Hank Jones, fresh from West Point and eager for combat experience, volunteers to lead, though he must convince a sceptical Winters. Also assigned to the patrol is Private David Webster, back in Easy after rehabilitation of an injury. While successful, the mission costs a soldier's life, prompting Winters to ignore the order to send out a second patrol the next night.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: A patrol in a nearby forest discovers an abandoned Nazi concentration camp, still filled with emaciated prisoners. The local citizenry, unbelievably disavowing knowledge of its existence, is made to clean it up. Suddenly, news arrives from Berlin: Hitler is dead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. Despite the fact that VE (Victory in Europe) Day has passed, Easy faces the prospect of imminent deployment to the Pacific Theatre. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Lock-In, A Weather Girl And A Disgusting Habit
programme.desc: Mary has Sheldon and Missy attend an overnight church lock-in. With Billy also attending, George feels nervous that he could meet Brenda without anyone knowing. Mary also tries to avoid being alone with Pastor Rob while chaperoning the event. George takes Principal Petersen out and runs into Brenda anyway. George awkwardly leaves. Mary and Pastor Rob bond while smoking cigarettes outside. George sees them together and leaves without Mary knowing he was there. Missy tricks Sheldon into hiding in a closet, and he falls asleep there. Georgie meets a woman, Mandy, (Emily Osment), at the laundromat, who says she is 25. Claiming he is 21, he takes her on a date, which goes well. When Mandy reveals she is actually 29, Georgie keeps quiet about his own age.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When going to the cinema, Doug and Carrie can't decide on which film they want to see. They run into Deacon and Kelly and it turns out Kelly wants to see the picture Doug wants to see and Deacon likes the film Carrie likes. It turns out Doug and Kelly have more in common and they start doing more things together. So do Deacon and Carrie.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie has to go build houses in Hartford for work while Doug wants to watch Planet of the Apes all weekend with her. He follows her to Harford and finds out she had a change of plans that didn't involve him.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: An Expensive Glitch And A Goof-Off Room
programme.desc: Due to a four-hour gap between his classes, Sheldon is given his private dorm room. Running into fellow student Sam, she says he gets undeserved special treatment, and nobody likes him because of his arrogance. Talking with Dr. Sturgis about humans being social creatures, Sheldon decides to experiment to see if people will like him more. After letting Sam use the room to study, word spreads Sheldon has a free room. He allows couples to use it to study, unaware of why they want it. When his parents find out, they tell him people are using it to waste time, convincing Sheldon to stop lending it out. Meanwhile, a glitch in the slot machines lets June win $11,000. Unable to pay it, Connie is forced to let June become a partner in her casino business. June and Georgie support each other's ideas for the place. Connie tries to talk Georgie to her side, but he says Connie hates anything she doesn't think of herself. Georgie adds a small disco ball, further angering Connie.

programme.desc: When going to the cinema, Doug and Carrie can't decide on which film they want to see. They run into Deacon and Kelly and it turns out Kelly wants to see the picture Doug wants to see and Deacon likes the film Carrie likes. It turns out Doug and Kelly have more in common and they start doing more things together. So do Deacon and Carrie.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie has to go build houses in Hartford for work while Doug wants to watch Planet of the Apes all weekend with her. He follows her to Harford and finds out she had a change of plans that didn't involve him.

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: While trying to restart his chiropractic business, Alan stumbles on a money-making goldmine that involves lonely women and a clueless Walden.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden takes Jenny and Barry with him on a road trip to reconnect with his past while Alan and Gretchen's relationship hits a rough spot over Alan's assumed identity.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan finally comes clean with Larry about he and Lyndsey. The fallout has ramifications for both weddings, with a twist no one saw coming.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Pink Cadillac And A Glorious Tribal Dance
programme.desc: Mary runs into Mr. Lundy who has a pink Cadillac from selling Mary Kay makeup products. He talks Mary into selling for him. She initially gets one sale to the high school librarian Ms. Hutchins. Mr. Lundy talks Mary into selling to the women in her Bible Study group. Mary succeeds, but later has a guilt-ridden dream for doing so, and quits being a seller. Sheldon is on Spring Break from college when he hears about a three-day comic convention. Unable to get an adult to go with him, Sheldon decides to rebel and take a bus to the convention. Seeing someone from his local comic shop and his old science teacher Mr. Givens, they debate the merits of Lost in Space, Star Trek and The Next Generation. Sheldon enjoys the trip though gets in trouble afterwards for sneaking out.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Lot Of Band-Aids And The Cooper Surrender
programme.desc: Sheldon meets Oscar and Darren, undergrads in the dorm room next door to his. Finding they have many things in common like video games, Sheldon likes hanging out with them. Meanwhile, George's job is at risk due to parents who are unhappy with the football team's performance. George's meeting with them doesn't go well. A stressed-out George lets Sheldon stay overnight to play Dungeons & Dragons at the dorm without consulting Mary. Though annoyed, Mary is able to talk to George and give him a sincere hug when she learns what is bothering him. Mary later goes to get Sheldon herself, as he is about to vomit from too much candy and root beer. Also, after clothes shopping, Missy is made fun of at school and accidentally injures herself trying to shave her legs. Connie patches her up and promises to show her how to do it safely, cheering Missy up.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Lainey surprises everyone with her college announcement, prompting Barry to vow to follow in the footsteps of Doogie Howser MD and become a doctor while still in high school.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam tells a lie that lands him a prom date with an older classmate. Barry asks Erica to help make the dance a night to remember for him and Lainey.

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.sub-title: Poker? I Don't Even Like Her
programme.desc: Grace's lousy poker playing leads Will's friends to ban her from the game. Elsewhere, Karen uses a snippy socialite as a guinea pig for cosmetic surgery.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Grace frets that she and Will might drift apart like their similar friends, she impulsively buys an expensive piano which she hopes will cement their mutual bond and love for music.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Gone Before Her Time
programme.desc: A celebration of four female singers whose lives were tragically cut short - Amy Winehouse, Aaliyah, Lisa Lopes and Selena Quintanilla-Perez.

programme.title: Love To Love You, Donna Summer
programme.desc: Shaped by Summer's own reflections, the memories of close family, friends and colleagues, and filled with the sounds of Summer's songs, this film is an in-depth look at the iconic artist as she created the music that took her from the avant-garde music scene in Germany, to the glitter and bright lights of dance clubs in New York, to worldwide acclaim. Her voice and artistry became the defining soundtrack of an era. A deeply personal portrait of Summer on and off the stage, the film features a wealth of photographs and never-before-seen home video footage - often shot by Summer herself - and provides a rich window into the surprising range of her artistry, from songwriting to painting, while exploring the highs and lows of a life lived on the global stage.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Mumford & Sons is a British folk rock band formed in London in 2007; Hozier is, as the song goes, a man you don't meet every day.

programme.title: Canada's National Parks
programme.desc: Canada is the second largest country in the world and historically the first to recognize the need to protect the diversity of its landscapes and preserve them for future generations. The over 100-year-old concept of Canadian National Parks is instrumental in protecting the unique natural environment and habitats of native flora and fauna from human influence. This is a challenging task for the employees of Parks Canada, as they have to reconcile tourism and economic interests with nature. And then, of course, there is climate change. Whether and how these developments are putting a strain on sensitive ecosystems will be the main topic of the discovery series, which brings Canada's unique landscape and culture to life. Knowledgeable and charismatic protagonists, including scientists, First Nations people, environmental activists, artists and storytellers who have a special connection with the landscape guide the viewer through this journey.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Follow Michael Portillo as he experiences the thrill of global rail travel, visiting locations recommended by his historic guidebook, and uncovering stories from the past.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

Her voice and artistry became the defining soundtrack of an era. A deeply personal portrait of Summer on and off the stage, the film features a wealth of photographs and never-before-seen home video footage - often shot by Summer herself - and provides a rich window into the surprising range of her artistry, from songwriting to painting, while exploring the highs and lows of a life lived on the global stage.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo visits a station fit for royalty in Windsor, views the Maidenhead Railway Bridge an engineering triumph built by Isambard Kingdom Brunel to span the River Thames at Maidenhead, and tries his hand at collecting the mail 'Victorian style' on a steam-powered travelling post office.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo tastes a Victorian superfood in Alton, explores the fascinating Whitchurch silk mill, untouched for over 150 years, and tries his hand at driving a steam train on the challenging Watercress Line.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: To celebrate the bicentenary of the birth of John Ruskin, our winning artist will visit some of this Victorian art critic's most-loved Venetian architectural icons, to photograph, sketch and take artistic inspiration, before returning to the UK to create a landscape artwork that will be unveiled by Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell, and hang at RIBA's prestigious London HQ.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Presenters Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan return for the tenth series of Portrait Artist Of The Year. programme.title: The World According To Amazon
programme.desc: An exploration into Amazon's business models and practices, from its inception to present day.

programme.title: Food, Inc. America's Food Industry
programme.desc: An unflattering look inside America's corporate controlled food industry. The current method of raw food production is largely a response to the growth of the fast food industry since the 1950s. The production of food overall has more drastically changed since that time than the several thousand years prior.

programme.title: Joan Rivers: A Piece of Work
programme.desc: Spend a year in the life of the irreverent comedian and pop icon Joan Rivers, peeling away the mask of the legendary performer, laying bare both the struggle and thrill of living life as a ground-breaking female performer. With unprecedented, unguarded access, the film captures intimate scenes shot in Joan's 75th year - Joan at home, during holidays, performing stand up and acting in a play she wrote, gossiping with her fans, writing jokes and managing her career - interweaving them with home movies, stand-up routines, book recordings, personal photographs and archival footage to create a lush visual landscape and cinematic backdrop for the narrative as it unfolds. A unique look inside America's obsession with fame and celebrity - Joan's story is both an outrageously funny journey and a brutally honest look at the ruthless entertainment industry, the trappings of success and the ultimate vulnerability of the first queen of comedy.

programme.title: Stop Making Sense
programme.desc: Filmmaker Jonathan Demme captures the frantic energy and artsy groove of Talking Heads as they perform hits such as 'Psycho Killer' and 'Take Me to the River' at the Hollywood Pantages Theatre in 1983.

programme.title: Annie Leibovitz: Life Through a Lens
programme.desc: Annie Leibovitz has produced some of the most iconic images of the last 30 years and is, literally, our most influential woman photographer. She has shot the rich and famous, the profound and powerful, the exceptional and notorious. Her camera has documented the horrors of war - most recently in Sarajevo and Rwanda. Masterful at exposing her photographic subjects, Annie's own life has been private and protected. In this film, she made the decision to bare her artistic process, her personal journey and her delicate balancing of fame and family to the camera - a camera that is being vigilantly pointed by a filmmaker who is her younger sister. From her hectic studio to her idyllic farm, we will experience Annie's current work and the creation of her latest retrospective book; we will face her losses as well as her grand successes.

programme.title: John Waters: This Filthy World

programme.desc: Kim and Matt design their new home, and Kelly is deadset on leaving her mark. Tracy marries her dream man, but finds out he invited his Mum on their honeymoon.

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie kicks off Christmas with a range of delicious dishes to see us through the festive season. From crispy duck and noodles to short rib beef, veggie filo pie to decadent dessert.

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie shares more fabulous one-pan dishes: foolproof tray-baked salmon in a bag, super-easy tomato fritters that one can rattle out in a frying pan, and roasted rolled pork and risotto.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom shares his speedy method for the perfect roast lamb with salsa verde and minty potatoes. For dessert, he makes an apricot pie with almond crumble.

programme.title: Inside The Factory Christmas Special
programme.desc: Explore the fascinating factory processes and surprising history behind the UK's favourite festive treats. Gregg Wallace visits the world's largest mince pie factory, and discovers the challenge of producing 2,000 perfect pies every minute. Cherry Healey travels to South Wales, where factory workers make enough tinsel in a year to reach from their hometown to Hawaii. And Ruth Goodman finds out about the explosive history of Christmas crackers.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick Stein's journey of discovery brings him to the sunnier climes of the Mediterranean, where he explores the French-Catalan legacy of Roussilon on the foothills of the Pyrenees next to the Spanish border. This is the land that celebrates the best of mountain cuisine with its grilled sausages and snails together with the finest fish straight out of the region's famous self-contained sea lakes. What unites them is the local love for Aioli, the pungent garlic mayo, cherished in southern France. In his country kitchen, Rick cooks a hearty dish of pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James encourages everyone to experiment in the kitchen and makes amazing comfort food from everyday larder ingredients. He is joined by special guest Cyrus Todiwala.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James returns home to his native Yorkshire, where he is joined by good friend and multi-award-winning chef Brian Turner.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Highland Christmas
programme.desc: Dame Mary Berry travels to her mother's homeland of Scotland for a magical winter break. She is joined by friends Andy Murray, Iain Stirling and Emeli Sande.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the last leg of their route 66 odyssey, the bikers are going west to California, the golden state. Si and Dave have been invited by one of the youngest executive chefs in the country to learn how to make the best mashed potato in the world.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: High Country Farm
programme.desc: It's lambing season on Royalburn Station and they're coming thick and fast. Nadia and Carlos get to see just how much their increased flock has produced and how that affects their bottom line.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Together
programme.desc: Jamie uses one wonderful piece of steak, big enough for six people, to ensure extra flavour and ease of cooking. He serves it with beautifully crispy, garlicky potatoes that are twice cooked for extra crunch.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Cookbook
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver has been cooking Christmas for his family for 20 years. In that time, he's experimented with loads of different ways of cooking the classics. But this year, he wants to show us his ultimate recipes - the ones he's decided really are the very best for Christmas. These are the dishes he'll be cooking on the big day for his own family this year.

programme.title: Beautiful Baking With Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet Sear creates some stunning centrepieces. There's a fabulous multi-coloured pinata cake, a cheesy risotto cake and a stunning vegetable tart.

programme.title: High Country Farm
programme.desc: It's lambing season on Royalburn Station and they're coming thick and fast. Nadia and Carlos get to see just how much their increased flock has produced and how that affects their bottom line.

This is the land that celebrates the best of mountain cuisine with its grilled sausages and snails together with the finest fish straight out of the region's famous self-contained sea lakes. What unites them is the local love for Aioli, the pungent garlic mayo, cherished in southern France. But this year, he wants to show us his ultimate recipes - the ones he's decided really are the very best for Christmas. These are the dishes he'll be cooking on the big day for his own family this year.

programme.title: Ainsley's Market Menu
programme.desc: Ainsley is headed up north to the Global Food Market in Logan to experience one of the most culturally diverse markets. The challenge is on to turn this bounty of global produce into a market menu.

programme.title: High Country Farm
programme.desc: It's lambing season on Royalburn Station and they're coming thick and fast. Nadia and Carlos get to see just how much their increased flock has produced and how that affects their bottom line.

programme.title: River Cottage Christmas
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and the River Cottage team serve up an extra special festive menu with a twist this Christmas. Hugh is ready to celebrate Christmas with a renewed sense of the value of meat - but also the wonders of seasonal vegetables. This year all the meat comes from the most extensively-living, free-ranging animals of them all - wild game - and more specifically those that are viewed by many people as pests. The River Cottage chefs sharpen their knives in pursuit of wild boar, muntjack deer and greylag geese. Head gardener Mark Diacono and Hugh seek to make the most of their veg patch, even in the depths of winter, unearthing some Christmas favourites, as well as some heritage varieties from off the beaten path. Forager John Wright teams up with Pam 'the Jam' Corbin to make some luxury stocking fillers. And the Canteen hosts a '12 Tasters of Christmas' night, packed with ideas to create an array of canapes and little bites, perfect for that festive drinks party.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand joins Alison Hammond and Aisling Bea to celebrate cake week with unseen footage and a new batch of bakers. Tom Allen reviews audience bakes.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Festive Specials
programme.desc: Paul and Prue celebrate with the Baker Ghosts of Christmas Past, featuring bakers from each year of Bake Off. The festive special ends with a performance by the Citizens of the World Choir.

programme.title: The Great Kiwi Bake Off
programme.desc: A Christmas baking celebration with six New Zealand celebrities coming in to compete for their charities and the opportunity to win $10,000 for their charity.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom shares his speedy method for the perfect roast lamb with salsa verde and minty potatoes. For dessert, he makes an apricot pie with almond crumble.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: Gregg heads to the German capital for a 3 day trip. He gets to the heart of Berlin's food scene and gets involved in the beer culture. Between meals he explores the city's often turbulent past and indulges his love of the musical Cabaret!

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick Stein's journey of discovery brings him to the sunnier climes of the Mediterranean, where he explores the French-Catalan legacy of Roussilon on the foothills of the Pyrenees next to the Spanish border. This is the land that celebrates the best of mountain cuisine with its grilled sausages and snails together with the finest fish straight out of the region's famous self-contained sea lakes. What unites them is the local love for Aioli, the pungent garlic mayo, cherished in southern France. In his country kitchen, Rick cooks a hearty dish of pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James encourages everyone to experiment in the kitchen and makes amazing comfort food from everyday larder ingredients. He is joined by special guest Cyrus Todiwala.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James returns home to his native Yorkshire, where he is joined by good friend and multi-award-winning chef Brian Turner.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc rustles up simple, delicious dishes in his Oxfordshire kitchen.

programme.title: River Cottage Christmas
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and the River Cottage team serve up an extra special festive menu with a twist this Christmas. Hugh is ready to celebrate Christmas with a renewed sense of the value of meat - but also the wonders of seasonal vegetables. This year all the meat comes from the most extensively-living, free-ranging animals of them all - wild game - and more specifically those that are viewed by many people as pests. The River Cottage chefs sharpen their knives in pursuit of wild boar, muntjack deer and greylag geese. Head gardener Mark Diacono and Hugh seek to make the most of their veg patch, even in the depths of winter, unearthing some Christmas favourites, as well as some heritage varieties from off the beaten path. Forager John Wright teams up with Pam 'the Jam' Corbin to make some luxury stocking fillers. And the Canteen hosts a '12 Tasters of Christmas' night, packed with ideas to create an array of canapes and little bites, perfect for that festive drinks party.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is sharing four more mouthwatering recipes. His succulent lamb stew is so quick to prep, but with seriously deep flavours; and a fast and furious tasty warm lentil salad.

programme.title: Gino's Italy: Secrets of the South
programme.desc: Gino travels on his Vespa, further South along Puglia's heel to Salento, riding along its Adriatic coast to the port of San Foca.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in France at an enormous foundry where they produce a cast iron pot every five seconds. He follows production of casserole dishes, from the arrival of 20 tonnes of crude iron right through to brightly coloured orange pots. Along the way he tests his mettle by taking a sample of molten iron at 1,550 degree Celsius. With only a heatproof visor and gloves as protection he dips a ladle in to a bubbling cauldron and pours the white-hot sample into a tiny mould. He also discovers that the coloured enamel they protect their pots with is made from glass.

programme.title: Find Me A Beach House
programme.desc: Michael and Jade are looking for their dream beach home in beautiful Byron Bay in northern New South Wales. They are enjoying early retirement and want to be close to the beach so they can go there each day with their young family.

programme.title: Find Me A Beach House
programme.desc: Noela and Ian love spending time with their grown up daughters and six grandchildren and are looking for their dream beach home on Melbourne's Mornington Peninsula in Rosebud. It will need to be close to the beach, shops and a bus stop.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Don't Hate Your House
programme.desc: Drew and Jonathan urge their parents to move from Banff to Los Angeles, but delays leave them in guest spaces. The brothers race to finish an ADU to provide their parents a settled home.

programme.title: Home Town
programme.desc: A woman inherits her mom's house, but it's far too small for her growing family. Ben and Erin come up with an ambitious plan to double the size of the house, adding a master suite and two additional bedrooms upstairs.

programme.title: Home Town
programme.desc: Young restaurateur brothers want to open a cafe near downtown with an apartment on the property. Ben and Erin give their choice a functioning cafe kitchen, beautiful cafe dining rooms and an incredibly efficient home.

programme.title: Home Town
programme.desc: A couple look for a house where they can entertain friends in a walkable neighborhood; Ben and Erin give their pick a complete facelift by adding a coastal colour scheme, built-in banquet and a back patio.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Kristin and Morgan are too cramped in their duplex rental and want to find a spacious dream home, so turn to Drew and Jonathan Scott for help.

programme.title: Rock the Block
programme.desc: The teams sprint to add the most value in their final spaces before a winner is named and redemption earned. Teams also tour each other's houses for the first time.

programme.title: Bryan's All In
programme.desc: Bryan helps Ryan and Taylor transform their live-and-work farm into a distillery and bar. In a town known for the hippest food outside the city, it's a race to open before tourist season.

programme.title: Christina on the Coast
programme.desc: Christina helps homeowners Jenny and Tobias renovate their cramped house for their family of five. While it has a stunning view, the home needs a functional layout.

programme.title: Farmhouse Fixer
programme.sub-title: East Coast Farmhouse, West Coast Vibes
programme.desc: It's the "newest" renovation yet as Jon Knight tackles a 50-year-old Colonial reproduction. The home needs major help, so Jon and Kristina bring in a bright West Coast design, adding much needed natural light, a vaulted ceiling and a dream mudroom.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's Renovate Don't Relocate
programme.desc: After twenty years living and working abroad, Mel and Iwan bought a country house in Surrey and decorated it in traditional style. But two spaces went untouched: a 1970's kitchen with no space to eat or socialise and a dark breakfast room now home to their rescue dogs. Cue Sarah who installs monitoring cameras, creates life-size floor plans and generates a contemporary layout and design. Can they successfully build a modern space that works with the rest of their home?

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's Renovate Don't Relocate
programme.desc: Hannah, an actor and Rob an aircraft engineer rented out their Victorian two bed house when Rob was offered work in the US. They returned after three years to start a family but realised that their home was no longer suitable. They eat on the floor from a coffee table and use the front room as a dumping ground. Worst of all, the sole bathroom can only be accessed via the room earmarked to become the nursery. Cue Sarah who installs monitoring cameras that generate design solutions on a budget.

programme.desc: Bryan helps Ryan and Taylor transform their live-and-work farm into a distillery and bar. In a town known for the hippest food outside the city, it's a race to open before tourist season.

programme.desc: Drew and Jonathan urge their parents to move from Banff to Los Angeles, but delays leave them in guest spaces. The brothers race to finish an ADU to provide their parents a settled home.

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: In this episode London based Karen Boswell struggles to afford her own home, so she decides to design and build a stylish houseboat, which she plans to moor near the Grand Union Canal. She's gone to town on her modern industrial bathroom design, indulging in a set of twin showers as well as a bath. Karen's story is combined with Marsha Olson's. Fed up with her cramped city apartment, sportswear manager Marsha sets out to build a spacious two story floating home on the idyllic Multnomah Channel near Portland, Oregon with an open living space and unobscured views of the river. Father and daughter build team, Ryan and Aime have never built a floating house before, and are doubly challenged when they're hit by an onslaught of snow, ice and epic flooding.

programme.title: House Hunters International
programme.sub-title: Expanding Horizons On A Shrinking Income
programme.desc: A young couple relocate, so the lady can get her master's degree, and the man can work on a novel in Madrid. She wants the quintessential Spanish flat in the city centre.

programme.title: The Best Of Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil and Kirstie catch up with two couples looking for their forever family home. Sunee and Will are desperate to put down permanent roots in London, while Alison and Phil are moving from Bolton to rural Herefordshire.

programme.title: The Best Of Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil and Kirstie catch up with house hunters who were looking for their forever family home. Chae and Corrina were searching on Scotland's East coast and with Jo in the East Midlands.

programme.desc: A pair of US service members are ready to put down roots. They're currently in South Korea and won't be able to see their house, so the pressure is on Mika and Brian to deliver their dream home.

programme.desc: A California couple moved their family of five to Arkansas, but the '90s rancher they purchased needs a major makeover before they can move in.

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home
programme.desc: A Massachusetts couple find hope and happiness with an inheritance and look to relocate. David helps them begin a new chapter by searching for homes in the town of Bath, Maine, including an eight-bedroom mansion.

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home
programme.desc: A couple scratched up a sweet $150,000 win, and now they're ready to blend their two big families together. With seven kids between them, including two sets of twins, David is tasked with finding a family home large enough for nine in Akron, Ohio.

programme.title: Flip or Flop
programme.desc: Tarek and Christina find a clean house in good shape. But when they return as excited new owners a few days later, they are floored by what they discover.

programme.title: Flip or Flop
programme.desc: Tarek and Christina spot a small listing in Buena Park, CA, that sits on a large lot. With no time for close inspection, they place an offer before they really know what they're getting into.

programme.desc: It is the final at Eyewitness Works in Sheffield, where developers Adam and Tim are about to award a mortgage-free apartment to the winner of this epic competition.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Audrey and Jeff, who dream of living in one of west London's most exclusive areas. However, the way they can afford it is to go underground. Their plan is to transform a listed Edwardian artist's studio - along with 5000-square-feet of derelict basement - into a bespoke subterranean home, complete with super-size kitchen, mezzanine sitting room, four ensuite bedrooms, a gym, cinema, steam room and wine cellar. As if the task weren't tricky enough, they are doing it without proper architect's drawings and Audrey is project managing - and the difficulties begin almost from the start.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: A bubbling and cracked wall throws up problems during Becky and Mark's guest room renovation. Edward battles the heat to create a surprise family terrace for Anna, and May has to learn new garden construction skills.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Richard and Debby have been good friends for over 20 years and have decided to join forces and buy their ultimate dream home together. They have a combined budget of 700,000 but they can't agree on where this property should be. Richard's heart is in Poole while Debby has always wants a change of pace in La Rochelle in South West France. Help is at hand with Jasmine Harman who delivers three fantastic homes in Poole, but can she clinch it for the UK as Laura Hamilton gives Jasmine a run for her money with three jaw-dropping French homes.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: With retirement approaching, Lesley and Ken have downsized their UK house to buy a holiday home on the Caribbean island of Barbados. Craig Rowe shows them what their budget can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Jane and Neil look for a holiday home around the Spanish seaside town of Gran Alacant for their 100,000 pounds. Can they find the perfect place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lian and Dave are searching for a holiday home in Spain's Almeria province to enjoy with their 13-year-old son, Dominic.

programme.title: Love It or List It Vancouver
programme.desc: Danielle loves Trevor but not his house. Can Jillian convince her to stay by bringing out the beauty in this heritage home? Or can Todd help Trevor move to a house with an outdoor space that fulfils his dream?

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home
programme.desc: A Massachusetts couple find hope and happiness with an inheritance and look to relocate. David helps them begin a new chapter by searching for homes in the town of Bath, Maine, including an eight-bedroom mansion.

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home
programme.desc: A couple scratched up a sweet $150,000 win, and now they're ready to blend their two big families together. With seven kids between them, including two sets of twins, David is tasked with finding a family home large enough for nine in Akron, Ohio.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Don't Hate Your House
programme.desc: Drew and Jonathan struggle with their LA home for parents Jim and Joanne. Delays spark guilt and anxiety as the dream home slips away, pushing the brothers to navigate high-stakes renovations.

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Adventurous young professionals, Ani Ridley and Charles Anderson are taking on the project of a lifetime - they've bought a busted old oil barge, the Dutchess 1910, that they want to turn into their rustic first home. Based on the Thames in Hampton, south west London, the couple have a tight budget which leaves no option but to do nearly all the work themselves. With no experience, are they biting off more than they can chew? Can they make some big savings by trying to make the stove, interiors and wheelhouse themselves?

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Home
programme.desc: Lisa and Jay are empty-nesters and found a house in a great neighbourhood. However, the dining room is closed-off and the outdated kitchen is dark and lacks proper storage.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Jane and Neil look for a holiday home around the Spanish seaside town of Gran Alacant for their 100,000 pounds. Can they find the perfect place?

programme.title: Property Brothers: Don't Hate Your House

programme.title: Chateau DIY

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Home
